title: EEG: Improvisation and Musical Structures
description: This dataset contains EEG recordings from musicians listening to chord progressions with varying harmonic structures. Participants were presented with four types of chord progressions (ii-IV-I, ii-V-I, ii-IV6-I, ii-V6-I) in an oddball paradigm, where deviant trials differed from standard trials in the middle chord through either exemplar (inversion) or functional (different harmonic class) manipulations. The study investigates how improvisation experience influences the neural processing and categorization of musical structures.
methods_description: EEG was recorded while musicians listened to chord progressions consisting of three chords, each lasting 400 ms in piano timbre with 400 ms inter-trial silence (total trial length 1600 ms). Eight experimental blocks were presented per subject, each containing 180 trials with standard progressions and deviant trials (15% probability) that varied in harmonic inversion or function. The key of each progression was randomized across trials.

funding_references.funder_name: Presidential Scholars in Society and Neuroscience program at Columbia University

funding_references.award_title: Presidential Scholars in Society and Neuroscience program
